prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial begins with a theatrical-style title card on a dark background. Large stylized lettering reads “THE WIZ,” surrounded by images of costumed performers. Smaller visible text includes “Dolby Stereo” and “MIDLAND 3,” with “1228 MAIN” below it. The layout resembles a cinema or show advertisement, with bright yellow, orange, and white lettering against black.

The image then cuts to a black screen. No visual content is visible apart from the archival watermarking.

After a long gap in the sampled frames, the scene changes to a close-up of a bathroom or washing setting. Two white basins or bowls are positioned side by side on a countertop. Hands are visible above them, holding dark circular objects, possibly bars or dishes, but the exact objects are unclear from the frame. The surrounding area appears reflective and bathroom-like, with fixtures or containers partly visible at the edges.

The commercial then cuts to a clean product display. A wrapped bar of Zest is centered on a bright, pale surface. The package is teal/green with curved orange and red bands, and the large brand name “Zest” is clearly visible. Smaller text on the package reads “deodorant beauty bar.” A white daisy-like flower is placed to the right of the product, contributing to a fresh, clean visual style. A pale container or bathroom item is faintly visible to the left.

In the final frame, the same product shot remains, but a large black caption appears at the bottom reading: “Cleaner than soap.” The Zest package stays centered, with the flower still visible to the right. The frame presents the product and written claim as the closing visual emphasis.
